concerns.text: Task T031B (Consumer) is listed after T030 (Test) and T029 (Report), but T031B explicitly consumes results from T031A (Producer). While T031A is listed before T031B, the grouping of T030 (a test that asserts the >15% MAE increase) before the implementation of the logic (T031A) creates a semantic ordering violation. T030 cannot verify a condition that T031A has not yet computed. T030 must follow T031A.

Task T029 (Report Generator) is listed before T031A (Statistical Logic). T029 generates the 'cost of biological plausibility' curve which requires the statistical comparison results (MAE differences, p-values) produced by T031A. The report cannot be generated before the data it visualizes is computed. T029 must follow T031A.

Task T036 (Rule Runner) is listed before T035 (Irreducibility Detector). T036 executes the enumeration pipeline and logs results, but the 'Rule Space Findings' section in T037 (and the logic in T036 itself) depends on the output of T035 to identify 'simplest rules' or 'irreducibility'. The detector (T035) must be implemented and available before the runner (T036) can utilize its logic to filter or log specific findings.

Task T042 (Report Update) is listed after T041 (Data File Creation), which is correct, but T040 (West Analyzer) is listed before T039 (Scaling Data Extension). T040 fits a power-law model to 'finer granularity' data. If T039 is the task that generates this finer granularity data (by extending the scaling script), then T040 cannot run until T039 completes. The current order implies T040 runs on existing data, but the description of T040 explicitly references the data generated by T039's scope.

Task T007 (Microcircuit) is marked [P] (parallel-safe) alongside T006 (Baseline Transformer). However, T007 implements 'full implementation... with fixed connectivity masks' which is a heavy computational artifact. While they are in different files, marking a complex model implementation as [P] alongside a baseline might mislead parallel execution strategies if they share heavy initialization resources (e.g., large tensor allocations) in a constrained 7GB RAM environment. Consider removing [P] or adding a note about resource isolation.
